title: carex × biharica
intro: Carex × biharica, also known as Hungarian water sedge, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Carex × biharica include treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Carex × biharica are typically made by infusing the roots and rhizomes in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant in a tincture or ointment form to apply topically.
